evidence: Separated AWS facts, AWS guidance, conditional derived recommendations, scenario requirements, distractor misconceptions, and learner-safe connective prose.
Cross-checked both lessons and all five items for consistent SCP, identity, audit, configuration, delegated-administration, ownership, and Region semantics.

Checked management-account-only exceptions, SCP all-features and subject limits, root-to-account evaluation, CloudTrail Region/opt-in behavior, Config source enablement, Security Hub home/linked Regions, RAM supported-type and Region conditions.
Tried alternate answer interpretations, hidden assumptions, key cues, unsupported absolutes, overbroad examples, and duplicate judgments; the recorded pre-audit revisions address the concerns found.
Used only the approved conditional recommendations and their exact premise claims; no new architecture recommendation or service behavior was introduced.
Bound all lesson/question support to exact approved current claim digests and retained applicable Region, account, configuration, and freshness qualifications.
Reopened every official AWS page cited by the two lessons and five questions on 2026-07-19; authored assertions remain projected through approved claims, not direct model memory.

author_revisions_made.disposition: Resolved in question revision 2 and a subject-neutral validator regression test.
author_revisions_made.summary: The first select-two matrices represented each keyed action as satisfying every requirement instead of showing their complementary roles.

author_revisions_made.disposition: Replaced with a coherent one-account/tagging alternative in revision 2.
author_revisions_made.summary: The first account-boundary question included an implausible OU-below-account distractor.

author_revisions_made.disposition: Removed or narrowed both phrases in lesson revision 2.
author_revisions_made.summary: Two lesson phrases asserted operational-overhead effects not stated by the approved claims.
